1. Did the applicant include a Project Narrative? [ X ] Yes [_] No

4. New and Competing Supplement applicants: Does the application include Attachment 12: Operational Plan? [ X ] Yes [ _] No [_] N/A
5. New applicant: Is the applicant a private, nonprofit entity or a public agency (including Tribal, and urban Indian organizations) in the United States? Suggested Resource(s): Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Agency Status [" [ X ] Yes [ _ ] No [_] N/A
6. Does the applicant propose to perform a substantive role in the project (i.e., does not apply on behalf of another organization)?
Suggested Resource(s): Budget Narrative[" | Attachment 2: Bylaws[] | Form 5A: Services Provided[] | Form 8: Health Center Agreements[
[ X ] Yes [_] No
7. Does the applicant propose to operate a health center that makes all required primary health care services available and accessible in the service area, either directly or through established arrangements, without regard for ability to pay? (Note that applicants may not propose to provide ONLY a single service or any subset of the required primary health care services.)
Suggested Resource(s): Project Narrative [] | Form 5A: Services Provided[" [ X ] Yes [_] No
8. Does the applicant propose to make General Primary Medical Care available directly (Column I) and/or through formal written contractual agreements in which the health center pays for the service (Column II)?
Suggested Resource(s): Form 5A: Services Provided [
[ X ] Yes [_] No
9. Does the applicant propose to make services accessible to all? If a sub-population is targeted (e.g., a school-based site that targets school-aged children), will health center services be available and accessible to others seeking services at the proposed site(s)?

The applicant did not maintain the current funding distribution across the special medically underserved population types listed for the service area.
15. Does the applicant propose to serve patients for each population type listed in the SAAT? Suggested Resource(s): SF-424A[" | SAAT[" | Form 1A: General Information Worksheet[" [ X ] Yes [ _] No [_] N/A

· Service area zip codes where the zip code patient percentages on the SAAT total at least 75 percent; or
. All service area zip codes if the sum of all zip code patient percentages on the SAAT is less than 75 percent?

The applicant did not include zip codes where at least 75% of current patients reside.
